channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Stall as Trump Cancels Envoys Pakistan Trip
item.description: President Trump cancelled a planned trip by envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying Iran improved its offer but not enough. Tehran says it will not be coerced into a deal through pressure or threats.

item.title: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Gunshots
item.description: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were rushed out by Secret Service after a loud noise and shots fired at the Washington Hilton. A suspect is in custody and Trump later returned safely to the White House.

item.title: Islamic Militants and Separatists Claim Sweeping Attacks Across Mali
item.description: Gunfire was heard near Malis international airport in the capital Bamako early Saturday. Coordinated attacks by gunmen targeted military positions across the West African nation.

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, hit multiple regions including occupied Crimea
item.description: A fire erupted at an oil refinery in the Russian city of Yaroslavl as Ukrainian drones attacked multiple Russian regions overnight. Strikes also targeted occupied Crimea, marking an escalation in Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: South Korea and Japan face renewed nuclear deterrent debate as Iran war reshapes security calculations
item.description: Seven in ten South Koreans now support acquiring nuclear weapons, while Japan's PM refuses to rule out the option. The Iran war has brought the long-simmering debate over nuclear proliferation in US allies to a boil, with IAEA sounding the alarm.

item.title: Germany deploys minesweeper to Mediterranean for possible Strait of Hormuz mission
item.description: Germany's defense minister announced the deployment of a minesweeper to the Mediterranean Sea in preparation for a potential mission to the Strait of Hormuz, as European nations consider naval operations to secure the vital oil transit route.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire agreement
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed six people in southern Lebanon, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The strikes come amid accusations of Hezbollah ceasefire violations and rising tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border.

item.title: Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner after security incident, shooter reported dead
item.description: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired outside the venue. The Secret Service neutralized the shooter, who was reported dead. Vice President Vance and several Cabinet secretaries were also evacuated.

item.title: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Shooting
item.description: President Donald Trump was evacuated by Secret Service after a shooter opened fire at the White House Correspondents Dinner in Washington. The shooter was killed by security forces. No injuries to Trump were reported.

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoy Trip to Pakistan
item.description: The latest US-Iran ceasefire talks appeared to fail before they began, with Iranian FM Araghchi leaving Pakistan and Trump saying he told envoys Witkoff and Kushner not to travel, citing too much time and work wasted on traveling.

item.title: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Strains
item.description: Israeli PM Netanyahu ordered the military to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon after at least six people were killed in Israeli strikes. Cross-border exchanges have strained the extended ceasefire agreement.

item.title: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 as President Blames Rebel Dissidents
item.description: A bombing on a Colombian highway killed at least 14 people. President Gustavo Petro blamed the attack on rebels linked to dissident FARC factions. The attacks come one month before the presidential election.

item.title: Al Qaeda affiliate launches coordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: An al Qaeda affiliate and Tuareg rebels claimed coordinated attacks on army bases across Mali in one of the boldest insurgent operations against the military-led government.

item.title: Russian forces pound Ukraine with massive drone and missile barrage
item.description: Russian forces attacked southeastern Ukraine including Dnipro with waves of drones and missiles, killing at least 10 people and injuring dozens in one of the largest attacks of the war.

item.title: Trump cancels envoys trip for Iran talks, says we have all the cards
item.description: President Trump scrapped a planned trip by Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying the talks involved too much travel and expense.

item.title: Macron reaffirms efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies warns of shortages
item.description: The French president said he is focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

item.title: Germany Blames Russia for Global Cyber Campaign Targeting Signal and WhatsApp
item.description: Berlin believes Russia is behind a global cyber operation targeting encrypted messaging apps Signal and WhatsApp, according to Der Spiegel, marking an escalation in Russian cyber warfare tactics.

item.title: Macron: EU Joint Defense Clause Is Stronger Than NATOs Article 5
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is steadier than NATOs Article 5, pushing for greater European strategic autonomy amid questions about US reliability.

item.title: Washingtons Resentment Toward Paper Tiger NATO Growing
item.description: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity during the Iran war, with frustration particularly strong toward Spain, France, and the United Kingdom over their perceived lack of support.

item.title: Japans Accelerated Remilitarization Raises Regional Concerns
item.description: Japans quickened remilitarization under the Takaichi administration, including eased restrictions on exporting lethal weapons, is posing grave threats to neighbors and requires international vigilance, analysts warn.

item.title: Ukraine Exports Drone Warfare Expertise to Allies
item.description: Ukraines hard-won drone warfare knowledge is being shared with allies, as three Ukrainian drone operators reveal how the country has become a global leader in unmanned aerial warfare technology.

item.title: Colombia explosives attack kills at least 13
item.description: An explosives attack in western Colombia killed at least 13 people and wounded 17 others, in one of the deadliest incidents amid escalating violence by FARC dissident groups.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: President Trump abruptly cancelled plans to send envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran, saying "too much time" had been "wasted on traveling" and that Iran had "tremendous infighting."

item.title: Macron says EU defence clause stronger than NATO Article 5
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual assistance clause was "stronger" than NATO Article 5, citing recent military aid to Cyprus as proof of Europe ability to defend itself amid concerns over US reliability.

item.title: Ukraine marks Chornobyl 40th anniversary under shadow of war
item.description: Ukraine commemorated the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster amid fears that Russia four-year-old war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.
